rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture and add contract content only within the selected owner's valid scope.
Keep type-level contract content on the component type owner; keep implementation-level content only on the implementation owner when the content depends on implementation scope.
Do not move, rename, or alter existing structural elements such as features, subcomponents, connections, or properties while inserting annex content.
generic_rules: Match inserted content to the owning declaration's visible namespace and scope.
Avoid introducing references that require subcomponent-path visibility when preserving a higher-level owner.
Prefer minimal structural change: add annex material without reformatting unrelated model content.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AADL annex content uses `annex agree {** ... **};` attached to a component declaration.
Implementation-scope material must remain after the declaration elements it depends on, while type-scope material belongs on the type declaration.
anti_patterns: Do not place content on an implementation owner if it only needs feature-level scope from the component type.
Do not modify unrelated architecture, properties, or connections as part of annex insertion.
Do not introduce out-of-scope identifiers or implementation-only paths into a preserved higher-level owner.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal AGREE equation patterns and logical operator details
Scalar helper-type diagnostics for generated expressions
